name: Hip-Hop & Rap
description: Rap legends, classic albums, and hip-hop history

questions.q: What is the real name of the rapper better known as 50 Cent?

source.notes: Full name Curtis James Jackson III.

questions.q: Rapper Drake grew up in which country?

source.notes: Born Aubrey Graham in Toronto in 1986.

questions.q: In which city was rapper Tupac Shakur fatally shot in 1996?

source.notes: Shot September 7, 1996; died September 13 at age 25.

questions.q: Sean 'Diddy' Combs founded which major hip-hop record label in 1993?

source.notes: Home to The Notorious B.I.G., Faith Evans, and others.

questions.q: What is Eminem's well-known rapping alter-ego?

source.notes: Introduced on 'The Slim Shady EP' in 1997.

questions.q: Which rapper had a 1995 crossover mega-hit with 'Gangsta's Paradise'?

source.notes: Featured on the 'Dangerous Minds' soundtrack.

questions.q: What was the title of Snoop Dogg's 1993 debut album?
choices: Da Game Is to Be Sold

source.notes: Produced by Dr. Dre; debuted at No. 1 on Billboard 200.

questions.q: What was the title of Dr. Dre's 1992 solo debut album?

source.notes: Certified triple platinum; introduced G-funk to the mainstream.

questions.q: Nas's critically acclaimed 1994 debut album was titled what?

source.notes: Widely regarded as one of the greatest hip-hop albums of all time.

questions.q: James Todd Smith is the real name of which rapper and actor?

source.notes: Stage name stands for 'Ladies Love Cool James'.

questions.q: Dwayne Michael Carter Jr. is known professionally by which name?

source.notes: Signed to Cash Money Records at age 11.

questions.q: Which Kendrick Lamar album won the Pulitzer Prize for Music in 2018 — the first hip-hop album ever to do so?

source.notes: Awarded April 2018; first non-classical/non-jazz album to win.

questions.q: Eminem won an Oscar for Best Original Song for which track from the film '8 Mile'?

source.notes: 2002 film; Eminem's Oscar made him the first hip-hop artist to win in that category.

questions.q: What was the title of Jay-Z's 1996 debut album?

source.notes: Released on his own Roc-A-Fella Records.

questions.q: Coolio's 'Gangsta's Paradise' sampled which song by Stevie Wonder?

source.notes: From Stevie Wonder's 1976 album 'Songs in the Key of Life'.

questions.q: Flavor Flav, Chuck D, and Professor Griff are all associated with which political hip-hop group?

source.notes: Formed on Long Island in 1985; known for 'Fight the Power'.

questions.q: What was the title of 50 Cent's 2003 debut album, produced by Dr. Dre and Eminem?

source.notes: Sold over 12 million copies worldwide; includes 'In Da Club'.

questions.q: Christopher Wallace was known by which rap stage name (in addition to 'Biggie Smalls')?

source.notes: Killed in a drive-by shooting in Los Angeles in 1997 at age 24.

questions.q: Lauryn Hill rose to fame as a member of which hip-hop group alongside Wyclef Jean and Pras?

source.notes: Known for the album 'The Score' (1996).

questions.q: Alongside Run and DMC, who was the third member of rap pioneers Run-DMC, tragically killed in 2002?

source.notes: Real name Jason Mizell; shot in his Queens studio October 30, 2002.

questions.q: Which British singer-songwriter's song 'Thank You' was sampled heavily in Eminem's 2000 hit 'Stan'?

source.notes: Dido also appeared in the music video.

questions.q: In Wu-Tang Clan, GZA is known by what elevated nickname?

source.notes: Delivered the celebrated 1995 solo album 'Liquid Swords'.
